When you’re juggling back-to-back visits, squeezing in meals, and trying to keep everything on track, you need a mobile app that works with you — one that’s fast, intuitive, and built for the realities of delivering care in the field.
That’s why AlayaCare puts caregiver voices front and center. We listen closely to what’s working and what we can do better — so your agency can deliver quality care without the hiccups.
The next round of updates continues to reflect what we’ve heard from caregivers — especially around visibility, reliability, and ease of use.

**Offline mode enhancements** : Caregivers working in rural or low-connectivity areas will soon benefit from automatic syncing of key visit data ahead of time. This means fewer disruptions during home care visits, regardless of signal strength.

**New calendar view** : A simplified calendar layout will make it easier to see upcoming visits at a glance. Whether weekly, monthly, or daily, caregivers will be able to view and manage their schedules in the format that works best for them.
Both updates are designed to reduce friction, improve clarity, and support the realities of working in the field.

Taken together, the excerpts substantiate the concept of Pulsen Combine as the core planner delivering finalized plans to a mobile execution system that presents visits as a task list to caregivers.

executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the best practices for Swedish home care (hemtjänst) visit scheduling UX, summarizing the key findings on visual design, information hierarchy, and critical features for care planners.

key_ux_recommendations.description: A bulleted list of actionable recommendations for designing or improving a home care scheduling user experience, focusing on clarity, efficiency, and meeting the needs of planners, caregivers, and clients.

scheduler_calendar_view_essentials.description: Details the essential information a human scheduler needs to see immediately on a main calendar or Gantt-style view. This includes employee assignments, visit timelines, client names, travel time indicators, and unassigned visits.

at_a_glance_info.description: Key information immediately visible on a compact visit card, such as client name, visit time, critical task icons, and alert status.

detailed_view_info.description: Comprehensive information accessible upon interaction (e.g., in a modal or side panel), including full address, contact details, all task descriptions, and client notes.

status_indicators.description: Visual cues (e.g., color-coding, icons) for visit status like unconfirmed, in-progress, late, missed, or completed.

conflict_alerts.description: Prominent alerts for scheduling issues such as travel time conflicts, overlapping visits, or unassigned visits.

qualification_warnings.description: Indicators for mismatches between employee skills or certifications and the requirements of a specific visit or client.

client_specific_flags.description: Icons or tags to highlight important client-specific information, such as allergies, pets, special access needs, or risk warnings.

legal_frameworks.description: The primary legal acts governing interventions, mainly Socialtjänstlagen (SoL) and Hälso- och sjukvårdslagen (HSL).

guiding_model.description: The national model for needs assessment, such as Socialstyrelsen's 'Individens behov i centrum' (IBIC).

personal_care_tasks.description: Category for 'personlig omvårdnad' tasks like hygiene, dressing, and mobility support.

service_tasks.description: Category for 'serviceinsatser' tasks like cleaning, shopping, and laundry.

delegated_healthcare_tasks.description: Category for healthcare tasks delegated from nurses to care staff under HSL, such as medication administration.

system_name.description: The name of the home care scheduling system being analyzed (e.g., AlayaCare, Pulsen Combine, Tietoevry Lifecare).

scheduler_view_features.description: Key features and UX patterns of the main planning, calendar, or Gantt view for dispatchers.

visit_card_design.description: Description of the information hierarchy and layout on the system's visit cards or appointment tiles.

mobile_app_experience.description: Analysis of the user experience for caregivers using the system's mobile application in the field.

key_differentiators.description: Unique or notable features, such as AI-powered optimization, specific reporting capabilities, or integration patterns.

nordic_scheduling_system_analysis.description: Provides a comparative analysis of the UX patterns found in major Nordic home care scheduling systems. Each entry will detail a specific system's approach to its calendar/scheduler view, visit card design, information hierarchy, mobile experience, and key features for planners. Systems analyzed include AlayaCare, Phoniro, Tietoevry (TES/Lifecare), Pulsen Combine, and CGI (VIVA/SASS).

core_calendar_ux_principles.description: Outlines fundamental UX principles for calendar design, including layout and navigation best practices (e.g., easy switching between day, week, and team views), and establishing a clear visual hierarchy using typography, color, and spacing to manage information density.

interaction_design_for_efficiency.description: Describes key interaction design patterns that improve a planner's efficiency. This includes drag-and-drop scheduling, bulk editing, creating recurring visits, and the use of non-disruptive side panels for editing details instead of modal windows.

caregiver_mobile_ux_needs.description: Focuses on the specific UX requirements for caregivers using the system's mobile application in the field. This covers simplified calendar views, clear task lists for each visit, offline functionality for low-connectivity areas, and easy access to client information and reporting tools.

continuity_solution.description: UX features that promote assigning familiar staff to clients, such as historical assignment data or continuity scoring.

travel_optimization_solution.description: Description of features for optimizing travel routes, calculating realistic travel times, and minimizing staff transit.

skill_matching_solution.description: How the UX helps match employee skills, certifications, and language abilities with client needs and visit requirements.

workload_balancing_solution.description: Tools that help planners distribute work evenly among staff to prevent burnout and ensure fairness.

staff_management_and_communication_featu.description: Details UX features related to the management of the workforce. This includes views for employee availability, shift management, skill/competency profiles, and integrated communication tools for sending updates or notifications to field staff.

data_optimization_and_reporting_capabili.description: Explains how modern scheduling systems leverage data and AI to provide optimization and insights. This includes features for automatic route planning, workload balancing, and generating reports on key metrics like travel time, visit duration variance, and staff utilization.

system_integration_patterns.description: Describes the importance of and common patterns for integrating the scheduling system with other critical software in the municipal ecosystem, such as client record systems (e.g., Pulsen Combine Core), payroll systems (e.g., Heroma), and digital lock systems (e.g., Phoniro).

accessibility_best_practices.description: Outlines key accessibility considerations for scheduling UIs to ensure they are usable by people with diverse abilities. This includes support for keyboard navigation, sufficient color contrast, screen reader compatibility with ARIA labels, and scalable text.
